rescue_reasoning: Climate relevance is explicit in both fields: policy_statement: “establish a Climate Technology Inventory System… including local generation and adoption of global technologies”; verbatim_text: “Chile will have a Climate Technology Inventory System… [including] the adoption of existing technologies worldwide.” This is an enabling/technology-transfer inventory system, not a direct mitigation outcome (explains gate_failure_reason: “no Mitigation outcome token found”). Keep the row but change primary_category from Mitigation to Resource Efficiency (technology transfer/adoption enabling).
policy_statement: By 2021, establish a Climate Technology Inventory System to be transferred, including local generation and adoption of global technologies (IM2b-2021-inventory).
mechanism_description: Chile will develop a climate technology inventory system to support the transfer, local generation, and adoption of climate-related technologies.
primary_causal_pathway: Governance/coordination tool → ↑ technology transfer & adoption → ↓ emissions intensity of energy/industry/transport/agriculture → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Human/system change: Government creates and maintains an inventory/registry/platform that identifies, catalogs, and matches climate-relevant technologies with domestic needs and potential providers/users, reducing information and coordination barriers to adoption.  
(2) Mediating process: Improved information availability, standardization, and transaction facilitation lowers search costs and accelerates diffusion of cleaner technologies (and associated know-how), shifting investment and procurement toward low-emission options.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: Broadly, lower emissions through earlier and wider deployment of mitigation technologies (and in some instances also improved resilience, but that is not the dominant, mechanism-wide effect).
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is enabling faster uptake of climate technologies that, when deployed, reduce GHG emissions compared with baseline technologies.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: An inventory system primarily functions as an enabling measure to increase adoption of climate technologies, with the largest expected climate impact being downstream GHG reductions from mitigation tech deployment.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While the instrument is “governance,” its climate-relevant endpoint is not adaptation (hazard/exposure reduction) nor resource efficiency per se; it most generally enables emissions-reducing technology diffusion across sectors.  
3) Edge cases applied: If the inventory focuses on adaptation technologies (e.g., flood defenses, drought monitoring) the dominant effect could shift toward adaptation; however, the generic “climate-related technologies” framing and typical use of such inventories is to accelerate mitigation tech transfer (renewables, efficiency, electrification, low-carbon industry).  
4) Applicability to ALL instances sharing this mechanism: Any policy whose core mechanism is creating a technology inventory to enable transfer/adoption is best categorized by the downstream enabling of low-carbon technology deployment, i.e., mitigation as the dominant climate effect.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: Whether a specific policy instance is more focused on energy, transport, industry, or adaptation co-benefits requires policy-level evidence and is not assigned here.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If the inventory is explicitly restricted to adaptation/resilience technologies (or biodiversity/nature tech), the dominant pathway could become Adaptation or Nature-Based Solutions; likewise, if it targets efficiency-only measures, Resource Efficiency could be plausible—classification should be revisited with scope details.
key_indicators: “establish a Climate Technology Inventory System”; “to be transferred”; “includes the local generation”; “adoption of existing technologies worldwide”
co_benefits: Potential innovation/industrial development and capacity-building spillovers; possible adaptation co-benefits if the inventory includes resilience technologies.
instance_edge_case_notes: Could be construed as an information-disclosure tool (inventory/registry), but the phrasing implies a broader coordinating system for technology transfer/adoption rather than a narrow disclosure requirement; no explicit enforcement, finance, or procurement trigger is stated.
